description: Port LEMON 1.3.1's min_cost_flow_test.cc hand-constructed graphs into Go tests asserting known optimal costs and flows.
commit: test: port LEMON's min-cost-flow hand-constructed test instances
steps: Create lemon_test.go in the repo root (SPDX BSL-1.0, package mcf).

Fetch LEMON source as the authoritative reference: `mkdir -p /tmp/lemon-ref && cd /tmp/lemon-ref && curl -sfL https://lemon.cs.elte.hu/pub/sources/lemon-1.3.1.tar.gz -o lemon-1.3.1.tar.gz && tar xzf lemon-1.3.1.tar.gz`. The test file is at `/tmp/lemon-ref/lemon-1.3.1/test/min_cost_flow_test.cc`.

Identify every hand-constructed MCF instance in that file that is expressible under this library's constraints: fixed-supply at a single source and single sink, no lower bounds on arcs, integer costs, integer capacities. Skip any test case that uses general lower bounds or multi-source/multi-sink configurations (the public API does not support these).

For each supported instance, write a Go test function named `TestLemonPorted_<DescriptiveName>` that: (a) constructs the arcs slice with appropriate *uint256.Int capacities via `uint256.NewInt(u64)`, (b) calls Solve, (c) asserts err == nil, (d) asserts res.TotalCost equals the LEMON-declared optimal cost, (e) calls checkSolution. Each test function must cite the corresponding section of min_cost_flow_test.cc in a comment at the top (e.g. `// Ported from min_cost_flow_test.cc test1 graph, lines N-M`).

At minimum port: the small 7-node test graph LEMON uses as its primary fixture (`digraph_test.lgf`-driven case translated to hand-built arcs — LEMON specifies its cost/capacity literals near the top of min_cost_flow_test.cc); feasibility/infeasibility corner cases present in the file; a zero-supply/zero-demand sanity case (demand = 1 on a trivial 2-node graph).

If any LEMON fixture yields a cost that overflows int64 under our best-effort rule, skip the cost assertion for that case but still require err == nil and checkSolution to pass (document the skip in a test comment).
Add at least 4 distinct ported test functions. Do not bundle unrelated LEMON cases into one function.
acceptance_criteria: lemon_test.go contains at least 4 TestLemonPorted_* functions each citing the source location in min_cost_flow_test.cc.
Every ported test asserts expected TotalCost and invokes checkSolution.
Tests that would require unsupported features (general lower bounds, multi-source) are explicitly skipped or omitted with a comment explaining why.
